jokes.text: There is definitely a helicopter pad on the roof of this building... Every single one of these corporate buildings has a helipad on the roof.
jokes.setup: Charlie making confident statements about corporate building architecture
jokes.punchline: His bizarre certainty about helicopter pads existing everywhere
jokes.explanation: Establishes Charlie's conspiracy-minded character through absurd architectural certainties

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: There is definitely a helicopter pad on the roof of this building... Every single one of these corporate buildings has a helipad on the roof.

jokes.text: What is he escaping from? Secret tunnels then.
jokes.setup: Mac questioning the logic of helicopter escapes
jokes.punchline: Charlie immediately pivots to an even more absurd conspiracy theory
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Charlie's paranoid logic with economic delivery

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: What is he escaping from?

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Secret tunnels then.

jokes.text: Secret tunnels, a monorail system and a helipad.
jokes.setup: Building on previous conspiracy theories
jokes.punchline: Adding monorail to the increasingly ridiculous list
jokes.explanation: Solid escalation with the absurd specificity of 'monorail system'

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Secret tunnels, a monorail system and a helipad.

jokes.text: There's always some big boss man fat cat... that needs to be able to escape by helicopter.
jokes.setup: Mac supports Charlie's helicopter theory
jokes.punchline: The idea that corporate executives need escape helicopters
jokes.explanation: Builds on Charlie's absurdity with Mac's own paranoid worldview

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: There's always some big boss man fat cat... that needs to be able to escape by helicopter.

jokes.text: We're out and about a lot, bro. Get to the point.
jokes.setup: Corporate executive explaining their bar was unmanned
jokes.punchline: Mac's dismissive response to legitimate business criticism
jokes.explanation: Shows the gang's obliviousness to their own incompetence

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: We're out and about a lot, bro. Get to the point.

jokes.text: Paddy's Pub can't be bought... Sold!
jokes.setup: Mac giving passionate speech about not selling their identity
jokes.punchline: Frank immediately agreeing to sell after seeing the offer
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection with excellent timing. Frank's instant capitulation undercuts Mac's entire speech brilliantly

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Paddy's Pub can't be bought...

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: Sold!

jokes.text: I would think of Paddy's as an Enron-type situation. We're gonna cut and run, and we're gonna leave you out there broke and jobless.
jokes.setup: Discussion about Dee's role in the sale
jokes.punchline: Dennis casually comparing their bar to a massive corporate fraud
jokes.explanation: Dark humor with perfect character integration - Dennis's sociopathic casualness about screwing over Dee

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: I would think of Paddy's as an Enron-type situation. We're gonna cut and run, and we're gonna leave you out there broke and jobless.

jokes.text: Let's sell this piece-of-shit bar... See ya later, piece of shit!
jokes.setup: Charlie seeing the monetary offer
jokes.punchline: His immediate emotional flip from attachment to dismissal
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Charlie's mercurial nature and lack of loyalty

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Let's sell this piece-of-shit bar... See ya later, piece of shit!

jokes.text: I'd love to be wooed. I could go for some wood... We'll get some wood. We'll build something cool.
jokes.setup: Discussion about being 'wooed' by the corporate buyer
jokes.punchline: Charlie mishearing 'wooed' as 'wood' and building elaborate plans around it
jokes.explanation: Classic Charlie misunderstanding that drives him off on a tangent

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: I'd love to be wooed. I could go for some wood... We'll get some wood. We'll build something cool.

jokes.text: Don't give a shit.
jokes.setup: Dee asking what she's supposed to do now
jokes.punchline: Dennis's blunt, uncaring response
jokes.explanation: Economical character moment showing Dennis's callousness

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Don't give a shit.

jokes.text: Right across the street? Well, that is good to know. I love Starbucks.
jokes.setup: Friend explains why coffee shop is failing due to Starbucks
jokes.punchline: Dee immediately says she loves the competitor that's destroying her friend's business
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dee moment - completely self-absorbed and oblivious to others' feelings

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: Right across the street? Well, that is good to know. I love Starbucks.

jokes.text: I don't mind. I'm gonna grab a beer while you're doing that. Come get me and give me all my stuff, and I'll start.
jokes.setup: Being asked to fill out a job application
jokes.punchline: Dee's entitled assumption that someone else will do her paperwork
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing Dee's entitlement even when desperate

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: I don't mind. I'm gonna grab a beer while you're doing that. Come get me and give me all my stuff, and I'll start.

jokes.text: Something like where those American soldiers are raising that flag up in that one war. The war where we saved Japan. Oh, correct. Hiroshima.
jokes.setup: Dennis wanting a statue commemorating them
jokes.punchline: Their complete confusion about WWII history and geography
jokes.explanation: Shows their ignorance while maintaining their confident stupidity

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Something like where those American soldiers are raising that flag up in that one war. The war where we saved Japan.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Oh, correct. Hiroshima.

jokes.text: They make all our cars. They're our friends. Not my car. I buy American... American cars are pieces of shit. Everybody knows it.
jokes.setup: Mac defending Japan as allies and claiming to buy American
jokes.punchline: Dennis immediately calling American cars shit
jokes.explanation: Good character contrast and Dennis's casual dismissal of Mac's patriotism

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: They make all our cars. They're our friends. Not my car. I buy American...

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: American cars are pieces of shit. Everybody knows it.

jokes.text: Oh, get a job? Just get a job? Why don't I strap on my job helmet and squeeze down into a job cannon and fire off into job land where jobs grow on jobbies?
jokes.setup: Being told to get a job
jokes.punchline: Charlie's elaborate, nonsensical metaphor about job acquisition
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation with perfect Charlie logic. The 'jobbies' ending is chef's kiss absurdity. Highly quotable and became a meme.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Oh, get a job? Just get a job? Why don't I strap on my job helmet and squeeze down into a job cannon and fire off into job land where jobs grow on jobbies?

jokes.text: Gay man, for one... You're so big and tough. You do not come across as gay. You don't look like a gay guy. That's 'cause he's a bear.
jokes.setup: Corporate guy reveals he's gay, Mac and Dennis react with surprise
jokes.punchline: Dennis's matter-of-fact explanation about bear/twink gay culture
jokes.explanation: Sets up the extended gay terminology discussion, good character moment

dialogue.speaker: Corporate Guy
dialogue.line: Gay man, for one... You're so big and tough. You do not come across as gay. You don't look like a gay guy.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: That's 'cause he's a bear.

jokes.text: A twink is small and slender... like Mac... No. I'm too muscular. I would be a bear... Don't think so. Not hairy enough. Smooth.
jokes.setup: Dennis explaining gay terminology
jokes.punchline: Mac's insecurity about his classification in gay culture
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment showing Mac's insecurity about masculinity through gay terminology

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: A twink is small and slender... like Mac...

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: No. I'm too muscular. I would be a bear...

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Don't think so. Not hairy enough. Smooth.

jokes.text: A power bottom is a bottom that is capable of receiving an enormous amount of power... Actually, you got it backwards. A power bottom's generating all the power by doing most of the work.
jokes.setup: Mac trying to explain gay terminology
jokes.punchline: Dennis correcting him with equally absurd pseudo-technical explanation
jokes.explanation: Absurdist discussion treated with complete seriousness. Dennis's 'correction' is beautifully ridiculous

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: A power bottom is a bottom that is capable of receiving an enormous amount of power...

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Actually, you got it backwards. A power bottom's generating all the power by doing most of the work.

jokes.text: Speed has everything to do with it. The speed of the bottom informs the top how much pressure he's supposed to apply. Speed's the name of the game.
jokes.setup: Continued discussion of gay terminology
jokes.punchline: Dennis treating it like technical specifications
jokes.explanation: Continues the absurd technical discussion, Dennis's confidence makes it funnier

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Speed has everything to do with it. The speed of the bottom informs the top how much pressure he's supposed to apply. Speed's the name of the game.

jokes.text: if they start to get empty, I'll just grab some from other tables... Yeah, which makes my job easier... You actually have to follow the rules.
jokes.setup: Charlie explaining restaurant protocol to Dee
jokes.punchline: Dee's logic that making her job easier is the point
jokes.explanation: Shows Dee's selfishness and Charlie's temporary competence

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: if they start to get empty, I'll just grab some from other tables...

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: Yeah, which makes my job easier...

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: You actually have to follow the rules.

jokes.text: You're pretty weird, you make me feel uncomfortable, and you smell really bad... You say that, but I don't... I don't see it.
jokes.setup: Dee listing Charlie's obvious flaws
jokes.punchline: Charlie's complete lack of self-awareness
jokes.explanation: Perfect Charlie obliviousness to obvious character flaws

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: You're pretty weird, you make me feel uncomfortable, and you smell really bad...

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: You say that, but I don't... I don't see it.

jokes.text: I use the same bill for two tables, and I pocket the cash for the other one. That's stealing. I know.
jokes.setup: Dee explaining her double drop scheme
jokes.punchline: Her casual admission that she knows it's stealing
jokes.explanation: Dee's matter-of-fact attitude about theft is perfectly in character

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: I use the same bill for two tables, and I pocket the cash for the other one. That's stealing. I know.

jokes.text: These are the Yellow Jacket boys... my old street gang from the '50s.
jokes.setup: Frank introduces elderly men
jokes.punchline: Claims they're his street gang from 70 years ago
jokes.explanation: Frank's delusional past and the visual of elderly 'gang members'

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: These are the Yellow Jacket boys... my old street gang from the '50s.

jokes.text: Not for you, bitch.
jokes.setup: Mac asking if Frank has another gang jacket
jokes.punchline: Frank's blunt rejection
jokes.explanation: Classic Frank bluntness with perfect economy

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: Not for you, bitch.

jokes.text: I will have you know I am taking this place up the butt with a little scheme I like to call Dee's Double Drop.
jokes.setup: Dee defending her success at the restaurant
jokes.punchline: Mixing sexual metaphor with criminal scheme branding
jokes.explanation: Good callback with added sexual metaphor confusion

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: I will have you know I am taking this place up the butt with a little scheme I like to call Dee's Double Drop.

jokes.text: Things move very quickly here at the Oldies Rock Café.
jokes.setup: Dennis asking how Charlie became manager so fast
jokes.punchline: Charlie's corporate-speak explanation
jokes.explanation: Charlie adopting corporate speak is perfectly absurd

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Things move very quickly here at the Oldies Rock Café.

jokes.text: First of all, let me just apologize to you for never calling you back, ever.
jokes.setup: Dennis trying to get a job
jokes.punchline: His terrible attempt at charm by admitting he ghosted her
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dennis sociopathy - thinks admitting bad behavior is charming

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: First of all, let me just apologize to you for never calling you back, ever.

jokes.text: Gangsters don't sing! Ever hear of gangster rap?
jokes.setup: Mac objecting to the gang singing
jokes.punchline: Frank's terrible logic connecting gangster rap to their situation
jokes.explanation: Frank's misunderstanding of gangster rap vs. actual singing

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Gangsters don't sing!

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: Ever hear of gangster rap?

jokes.text: Those darn Yellow Jacket boys, Buzz, buzz, bumble, They don't pay for soda pop 'cause they really rumble
jokes.setup: Trying to be intimidating gang members
jokes.punchline: Their completely non-threatening doowop song
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion of gang intimidation through wholesome 1950s song

dialogue.line: Those darn Yellow Jacket boys, Buzz, buzz, bumble, They don't pay for soda pop 'cause they really rumble

jokes.text: While you've been picking up bar whores... I have been doubledropping like a bastard out there.
jokes.setup: Criticizing Dennis for flirting
jokes.punchline: Dee's pride in her criminal efficiency
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing Dee's misplaced pride in theft

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: While you've been picking up bar whores... I have been doubledropping like a bastard out there.

jokes.text: Don't you wanna be attractive to me? Come on.
jokes.setup: Manager criticizing Dennis
jokes.punchline: Dennis's narcissistic assumption that everyone wants to attract him
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dennis narcissism - can't imagine someone not wanting to attract him

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Don't you wanna be attractive to me? Come on.

jokes.text: Look, pal, you'd better not mess with us. You see Hawky over here? He's crazy. I'm not sure what he's capable of.
jokes.setup: Frank trying to intimidate the corporate guy
jokes.punchline: Threatening with an elderly man who immediately has a heart attack
jokes.explanation: Dark humor with perfect timing - threat immediately undermined by death

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: Look, pal, you'd better not mess with us. You see Hawky over here? He's crazy. I'm not sure what he's capable of.

jokes.text: What's my name? What is it? Beautiful... Oh, my God. You're a dick. I called you Beautiful all the time.
jokes.setup: Manager testing if Dennis knows her name
jokes.punchline: Dennis thinking 'Beautiful' is an acceptable substitute for her actual name
jokes.explanation: Classic Dennis move - thinking generic compliments substitute for basic human connection

dialogue.speaker: Manager
dialogue.line: What's my name? What is it?

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Beautiful...

dialogue.speaker: Manager
dialogue.line: Oh, my God. You're a dick.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: I called you Beautiful all the time.

jokes.text: Your friends and their doowop group... they showed up at my house and attacked me. One of them died on my doorstep, and it sort of put me in a funk.
jokes.setup: Explaining why he's firing everyone
jokes.punchline: Casually mentioning someone died and it put him 'in a funk'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy - death as mild inconvenience. Perfect deadpan delivery

dialogue.speaker: Corporate Guy
dialogue.line: Your friends and their doowop group... they showed up at my house and attacked me. One of them died on my doorstep, and it sort of put me in a funk.

jokes.text: But, no, you're not fired. I'm just kidding. You are.
jokes.setup: Seeming to spare the manager from firing
jokes.punchline: Immediately taking it back
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection with cruel efficiency

dialogue.speaker: Corporate Guy
dialogue.line: But, no, you're not fired. I'm just kidding. You are.

jokes.text: She is strung up supertight.
jokes.setup: Manager being upset about being fired
jokes.punchline: Dennis's casual dismissal of legitimate emotional reaction
jokes.explanation: Shows Dennis's inability to understand normal human emotions

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: She is strung up supertight.

jokes.text: Hawky never spoke much, But he had his doo-doo-doowop, Now you're buzzing around that hive in the sky
jokes.setup: Funeral for their dead gang member
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous doowop funeral song with bee metaphors
jokes.explanation: Perfect absurdist callback - maintains their doowop identity even at a funeral

dialogue.line: Hawky never spoke much, But he had his doo-doo-doowop, Now you're buzzing around that hive in the sky
